title: Red Blood Cells Oxygen
script_summary: Exploring the fascinating role of red blood cells in oxygen transport.

segments.voiceover: Here's what science actually says about red blood cells and oxygen.
Red blood cells are the body’s oxygen couriers, but how do they do it?
They contain hemoglobin, a protein that binds to oxygen in the lungs.
This binding process is crucial—without it, our cells would suffocate.
But did you know researchers are discovering even more about how blood cells adapt under stress?
These adaptations could change how we understand oxygen transport.
Want to dive deeper into the science of your body? Hit that follow!
